revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:筑前国続風土記]]
'''Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki''' (筑前国続風土記) is a {{ill|Chorography|en|Chorography|qid=Q2088593|lt=geographical record|1=Chorography}} compiled by the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|qid=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}. == Overview == The *Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki* was authored by the domain's {{ill|Ru (Confucian scholar)|ja|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ru (Confucian scholar)|lt=Ru}}, {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|en|Kaibara Ekken|qid=Q1721396|lt=Kaibara Ekiken|1=Kaibara Ekken}}, in 1688, with contributions from his nephew, {{ill|Kaibara Kōko|ja|貝原好古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaibara Kōko|1=Kaibara Yoshifuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q111291078}}, and his pupil, Takeda Sadanao. The compilation was completed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}}16 (1703) and submitted to {{ill|Kuroda Tsunamasa|ja|黒田綱政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Tsunamasa|1=Kuroda Tsunamasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678676}}, the 4th lord of the Fukuoka Domain. Subsequent revisions were made, and the work was finalized in {{ill|Hōei|en|Hōei|qid=Q1145262|lt=Hōei|1=Hōei}}6 (1709).<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki|url=https://www.lib.pref.fukuoka.jp/hp/gallery/H25/PDF/fudokikaidai.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Fukuoka Prefectural Library|accessdate=2021-09-17|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-08-06|qq=}}</ref> Field investigations throughout the villages of Chikuzen Province were conducted to ensure its accuracy. Later, when the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} encouraged similar compilations across domains, the linguistic style and descriptive methods of this record served as a model.<ref name=":0" /> Although various manuscript copies circulated during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the version preserved in the {{ill|Takeda Archive|ja|竹田文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Archive|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} includes 30 main volumes, supplemented by an additional 31st volume containing omissions and corrections. Takeda Sadanao's editing is evident in the final manuscript, which was transcribed by Matsunaga Tameyuki (Kyoshū), a samurai of the Fukuoka Domain.<ref name=":2" /> {| class="wikitable" |+ Based on the Takeda Archive version<ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|title=Takeda Archive *Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki*|url=https://www.lib.pref.fukuoka.jp/hp/gallery/H25/fudoki.html|publisher=Fukuoka Prefectural Library|accessdate=2021-09-17|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2024-08-06|qq=}}</ref> ! Volume !! Title !! colspan="7" | Content |- | 1 || Upper Preface || colspan="7" rowspan="2" | Foreword, historical overview of Chikuzen Province, statistics on rice yields, households, population, and geographical features such as rivers and mountains. |- | 2 || Lower Preface |- | 3 || {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} || colspan="7" | Details about Fukuoka Castle and its castle town. |- | 4 || {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11284628}} || colspan="7" | Historical and contemporary descriptions of Hakata, including its neighborhoods and shrines. |- ! ! | ! !巻 !表題 !巻 !表題 !巻 !表題 | |- | 5 || {{ill|Naka District, Ibaraki|en|Naka District, Ibaraki|qid=Q1062968|lt=Naka District|1=Naka District, Ibaraki}} (Upper) || 10 || {{ill|Yasu District|ja|夜須郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu District|1=Yasu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10933275}} || 14 || {{ill|Onga District, Fukuoka|en|Onga District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1156362|lt=Onga District|1=Onga District, Fukuoka}} (Upper) || 20 || {{ill|Sawara District, Fukuoka|en|Sawara District, Fukuoka|qid=Q7428600|lt=Sawara District|1=Sawara District, Fukuoka}} (Upper) || rowspan="6" | Descriptions of old landmarks, scenic locations, and shrines across the districts. |- | rowspan="2" | 6 || Naka District (Lower) || rowspan="2" | 11 || {{ill|Kami District|ja|上座郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami District|1=Jōza district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867428}} || 15 || Onga District (Lower) || 21 || Sawara District (Lower) |- | {{ill|Shikida District|ja|席田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikida District|1=Mushiroda District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11060220}} || {{ill|Shimo District|ja|下座郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo District|1=Geza district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10868527}} || 16 || {{ill|Munakata District, Fukuoka|en|Munakata District, Fukuoka|qid=Q6935606|lt=Munakata District|1=Munakata District, Fukuoka}} (Upper) || 22 || {{ill|Ito District|ja|怡土郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito District|1=Ito district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11072672}} |- | 7 || {{ill|Mikasa District|ja|御笠郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa District|1=Mikasa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071211}} (Upper) || rowspan="2" | 12 || {{ill|Kama District|ja|嘉摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kama District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || 17 || Munakata District (Lower) || 23 || {{ill|Shima District|ja|志摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shima District|1=Shima District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9384245}} |- | 8 || Mikasa District (Middle) || {{ill|Honami District|ja|穂波郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honami District|1=Honami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597062}} || 18 || {{ill|Omote Kasuya District|ja|表糟屋郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omote Kasuya District|1=Omotekasuya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626470}} || |- | 9 || Mikasa District (Lower) || 13 || {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}} || 19 || {{ill|Ura Kasuya District|ja|裏糟屋郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kasuya District|1=Urakasuya district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626780}} |- ! ! ! ! ! ! ! ! ! | | | | | | | | |- | 24 || Castles and Battlefields (Vol. 1) || 26 || Castles and Battlefields (Vol. 3) || 28 || Castles and Battlefields (Vol. 5) || || || rowspan="2" | Historical descriptions of castles and battle sites. |- | 25 || Castles and Battlefields (Vol. 2) || 27 || Castles and Battlefields (Vol. 4) || || || |- ! ! ! ! ! ! ! ! ! | | | | | | | | |- | 29 || Local Products (Vol. 1) || 30 || Local Products (Vol. 2) || || || || || Descriptions of local resources, extraction methods, and characteristics, including minerals, medicinal substances, birds, freshwater fish, and seaweed. |- | 31 || Addenda || || || || || || || Details on omitted topics. |} == Cultural Properties == === Fukuoka Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property === * '''Takeda Family Documents''' - Designated April 12, 1960 (Showa 35). Includes 8 scrolls, with *Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki* and {{ill|Kuroda Family Genealogy|ja|黒田家譜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Family Genealogy|1=Kuroda Family Genealogy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65294057}} among them.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties|url=http://www.city.onojo.fukuoka.jp/s077/030/010/030/002/20170801164208.html|publisher=Onojo City Cultural Property Division|accessdate=2021-09-17|qq=}}</ref> These documents were deposited in the Fukuoka Prefectural Cultural Center in 1971 and are now preserved by the {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Library|ja|福岡県立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Library|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592523}}. Among them is the Takeda Archive's edition of the *Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki*, a key resource passed down through the Takeda family, whose members served as Confucian scholars for the Fukuoka Domain.<ref name=":2" /> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.lib.pref.fukuoka.jp/hp/gallery/H25/fudoki.html Takeda Archive *Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki*] - View the original text at the {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Library|ja|福岡県立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Library|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592523}}. * [https://www.nakamura-u.ac.jp/institute/media/library/kaibara/archive05.html Kaibara Ekiken Archive: *Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki*] - {{ill|Nakamura Gakuen University|en|Nakamura Gakuen University|qid=Q6960230|lt=Nakamura Gakuen University|1=Nakamura Gakuen University}} hosts a modern typeset version.
<!--Chikuzen no Kuni Zoku Fudoki--> <!-- no translated page template found in history --> [[ja:筑前国続風土記]]
